title: Pilot light emissions omitted under federal exemption despite NY Part 253 override

issue.observed_condition: The equipment inventory and workbook omit pilot light sources and cite 40 CFR §98.30(d) as the basis for excluding pilot light emissions.
issue.expected_condition: For NY Part 253 reporting, pilot light emissions should be included or supported through engineering estimate/common-fuel-source aggregation.
issue.why_triggered: The system found a federal pilot-light exemption citation in the equipment inventory and no pilot-light source rows, engineering estimate, or common-fuel-source aggregation support.
issue.client_reported_basis_or_rationale: Pilot light emissions excluded per 40 CFR §98.30(d).

upstream_rule_results.message: Pilot light omission conflicts with NY Part 253 pilot-light inclusion requirement.

source_locations.source_snippet: Footnote: Pilot light emissions excluded per 40 CFR §98.30(d) - reporting not required for pilot lights.
Evidence Pack Index and OCR Routing Map lists equipment inventory and utility evidence IDs for downstream traceability.

linked_workbook_locations.displayed_value: Pilot lights excluded per 40 CFR §98.30(d) — not required to report GHG emissions from pilot lights.
No separate pilot light row appears in the Scope 1 emissions roll-up.

regulatory_citations.requirement_summary: Pilot light emissions must be included in the emissions data report.
regulatory_citations.applicability_explanation: The workbook cites the federal exemption, but this demo scenario is for NY Part 253 reporting.

regulatory_citations.requirement_summary: Federal reporting exempts pilot light emissions.
regulatory_citations.applicability_explanation: The company cites this federal basis as the reason for omission.

internal_basis: Formula library G-001 completeness ticket conversion

qualitative_factors: Omitted source class affects source completeness
materiality.rationale: The omission may be small quantitatively but is central to source coverage and jurisdiction-specific compliance.

remediation.recommended_action: Add pilot light sources or engineering/common-fuel aggregation support and recalculate affected Scope 1 emissions.
required_additional_evidence: Pilot light fuel-use estimate or common-fuel-source aggregation note
Updated equipment inventory including pilot light treatment
remediation.suggested_client_question: Please provide the method used to include pilot light emissions under NY Part 253 §253-2.7(j), or explain why no pilot lights exist for the listed boilers/process heaters.

audit_trail.note: Created from mock backend analysis response for demo UI development.

title: October natural gas volume is entered at 10x the source-bill quantity

issue.observed_condition: The Natural Gas worksheet records October usage as 281,000 MMBtu, while the October utility bill shows 28,100 MMBtu.
issue.expected_condition: The workbook monthly usage should reconcile to the traceable billed quantity for the same service period.
issue.why_triggered: Workbook-to-evidence reconciliation identified a 252,900 MMBtu overstatement in October.

upstream_rule_results.message: Workbook and evidence quantities do not reconcile.

source_locations.source_snippet: Usage 28,100 MMBtu for Oct 1, 2023 to Oct 31, 2023.

calculation_impact.impact_explanation: The 10x activity error propagates into CO2, CH4, N2O, and CO2e calculations.

regulatory_citations.requirement_summary: Individual fuel volumes should be reported and traceable to billing or meter evidence.
regulatory_citations.applicability_explanation: The October volume differs between the utility bill and workbook.

assurance_basis: Recalculation and reconciliation to source evidence
internal_basis: Formula library R-001 reported-versus-recalculated absolute difference
Formula library G-002 accuracy gap-ticket conversion

materiality.rationale: The activity mismatch is far above the demo materiality threshold.

remediation.recommended_action: Correct Natural Gas!D12 to the source-bill quantity and reperform affected monthly and annual totals.
remediation.suggested_client_question: Please confirm the correct October natural gas volume and provide the source used for the workbook entry.

audit_trail.note: Created from mock backend analysis response for demo UI development.

title: Boilers and diesel generator are aggregated across different source types

issue.observed_condition: The Emissions Summary aggregates natural gas boilers and the diesel emergency generator into one combined CO2e line.
issue.expected_condition: Aggregation should preserve source type/unit type distinctions so each source category and calculation method can be verified.
issue.why_triggered: The aggregation row combines BLR-001/BLR-002 boiler sources with GEN-001 RICE generator data under one line item.
issue.client_reported_basis_or_rationale: Natural Gas Boilers + Diesel Generator grouped in the emissions summary.

upstream_rule_results.message: Different stationary combustion unit types are aggregated together.

source_locations.source_snippet: BLR-001/BLR-002 are Unit Type: Boiler; GEN-001 is Unit Type: Reciprocating Internal Combustion Engine (RICE).
Aggregation worksheet groups BLR-001, BLR-002, and GEN-001 for 2023 annual roll-up.

regulatory_citations.requirement_summary: Aggregation of stationary combustion units should preserve unit/source-type constraints.
regulatory_citations.applicability_explanation: The summary combines boilers and a RICE emergency generator.

regulatory_citations.requirement_summary: Aggregation support should allow source-category-specific verification.
regulatory_citations.applicability_explanation: Part 253 incorporates federal stationary combustion reporting logic with NY-specific requirements.

assurance_basis: Calculation method traceability by source category

remediation.recommended_action: Split the Emissions Summary into separate boiler and generator lines, with separate calculation method and source trace.
required_additional_evidence: Updated aggregation worksheet by unit type
remediation.suggested_client_question: Please provide a source-level roll-up that separates boiler units from the diesel emergency generator.

audit_trail.note: Created from mock backend analysis response for demo UI development.

title: Natural gas emission factor applied to solid biomass months

issue.observed_condition: The Biomass Boiler worksheet identifies August and September fuel as Solid Biomass Residue but applies the natural gas CO2 factor 53.06 kg CO2/MMBtu.
issue.expected_condition: The emission factor and calculation method should match the actual fuel type and required biomass/biogenic treatment.
issue.why_triggered: Fuel type extracted from the workbook and BLR-003 records does not match the emission factor selected for the same rows.
issue.client_reported_basis_or_rationale: August and September rows retain the natural-gas factor despite biomass fuel type.

upstream_rule_results.message: Fuel type does not match the applied emission factor.

source_locations.source_snippet: BLR-003 August fuel type: Solid Biomass Residue.
BLR-003 September fuel type: Solid Biomass Residue.

linked_workbook_locations.displayed_value: Fuel Type = Solid Biomass Residue; CO2 EF Applied = 53.06 kg CO2/MMBtu

calculation_impact.impact_explanation: The calculation cannot be considered reperformable until the biomass method/factor and biogenic CO2 treatment are documented.

regulatory_citations.requirement_summary: Biomass fuel combustion and biogenic CO2 treatment require appropriate method selection.
regulatory_citations.applicability_explanation: August and September rows identify solid biomass but use natural gas factors.

regulatory_citations.requirement_summary: Fuel type must match the selected emission factor and method.
regulatory_citations.applicability_explanation: Natural gas Table C factor should not be applied to solid biomass residue months.

remediation.recommended_action: Replace the natural-gas factor with the appropriate biomass calculation method and separately document fossil versus biogenic CO2 treatment.
required_additional_evidence: Biomass fuel analysis and carbon-content support
remediation.suggested_client_question: Please provide the approved calculation method and factor support used for BLR-003 solid biomass residue in August and September.

audit_trail.note: Created from mock backend analysis response for demo UI development.

title: Biomass monthly composite lacks weekly sampling support

issue.observed_condition: The evidence pack contains a single end-of-period biomass fuel lab report but no weekly sample logs, composite preparation record, or sampling protocol.
issue.expected_condition: Partially biogenic fuel fractions should be supported by a weekly sampling chain and documented monthly composite preparation.
issue.why_triggered: The system found a biomass fraction/lab result without the supporting sampling-chain documents expected for the period.
issue.client_reported_basis_or_rationale: Single lab report used as support for biomass fraction.

upstream_rule_results.message: Biomass fraction lacks weekly sample chain and monthly composite support.

source_locations.source_snippet: Biomass Fuel Lab Analysis Report — sample date September 30, 2023; fuel description, carbon content, HHV.
BLR-003 September fuel record uses solid biomass residue.

regulatory_citations.requirement_summary: Partially biogenic fuels require support for biomass fraction determination, including sampling/composite documentation where applicable.
regulatory_citations.applicability_explanation: The evidence pack includes only a single end-of-period lab result.

assurance_basis: Occurrence and existence of underlying evidence
internal_basis: Formula library G-003 occurrence/existence ticket conversion
Expected evidence schedule for biomass sampling

qualitative_factors: Sampling-chain failure makes biomass fraction unverifiable
materiality.rationale: The issue is evidentiary rather than a directly quantified arithmetic difference.

remediation.recommended_action: Provide weekly biomass sample logs, composite preparation record, and sampling protocol for the month(s) with biomass combustion.
remediation.suggested_client_question: Please provide the weekly sample logs and composite preparation record supporting the biomass fraction used for BLR-003.

title: Missing December data filled without Part 253 §3.1 substitution method

issue.observed_condition: The Natural Gas worksheet uses an estimated December volume without documented Part 253 §3.1 substitution method support.
issue.expected_condition: Missing emissions data substitutions should follow and document the applicable §3.1 substitution method and rationale.
issue.why_triggered: The workbook indicates an estimated value while the expected missing-data method documentation is absent from the evidence set.
issue.client_reported_basis_or_rationale: Estimated — bill not received; simple average used.

upstream_rule_results.message: Estimated value lacks documented substitution method.

source_locations.source_snippet: December natural gas utility bill record requires service-period and substitution review.
Evidence index does not list a Part 253 §3.1 substitution worksheet or missing-data method support.

regulatory_citations.requirement_summary: Missing-data substitutions must follow prescribed methods and retain documentation.
regulatory_citations.applicability_explanation: The workbook uses an estimated December value without method support.

regulatory_citations.requirement_summary: Stationary combustion reporting must address missing data consistently with Part 253 missing-data procedures.
regulatory_citations.applicability_explanation: The evidence set lacks substitution documentation.

internal_basis: Formula library G-003 occurrence/existence ticket conversion

materiality.rationale: Impact cannot be quantified until substitution basis and correct actual bill are confirmed.

remediation.recommended_action: Document the Part 253 §3.1 substitution method or replace the estimate with actual billing evidence and rerun calculations.
required_additional_evidence: December actual bill or method documentation
remediation.suggested_client_question: Please provide the Part 253 §3.1 substitution method and rationale used for the estimated December natural gas volume.

title: Billing evidence covers only partial year

issue.observed_condition: The demo evidence scenario provides only January through March natural gas billing support for a full-year calculation.
issue.expected_condition: Annual natural gas volumes should be traceable to billing statements or equivalent records covering the full reporting year without undocumented gaps.
issue.why_triggered: Calendar-year completeness logic found only three monthly billing periods in the selected evidence set.
issue.client_reported_basis_or_rationale: Only available bills were provided at compilation time.

upstream_rule_results.message: Only partial-year billing evidence is available.

regulatory_citations.requirement_summary: Annual MMBtu reporting should be traceable to account-level billing or equivalent source records.
regulatory_citations.applicability_explanation: The selected evidence scenario supports only three of twelve months.

internal_basis: Evidence type J2-001 monthly utility bill coverage

remediation.recommended_action: Provide billing statements or meter records for all missing months or a complete annual supplier summary with account-level support.
required_additional_evidence: April-December utility bills or annual supplier account summary
remediation.suggested_client_question: Please provide the remaining 2023 natural gas bills or an annual supplier statement that reconciles to the workbook total.

audit_trail.note: Created from mock backend analysis response for demo UI development.

title: Cross-year natural gas bill allocated entirely to current reporting year

issue.observed_condition: The December evidence bill covers Dec 16, 2023 to Jan 15, 2024, but the workbook treats the quantity as a December 1-31, 2023 record without allocation support.
issue.expected_condition: A cross-year service period should be prorated or otherwise allocated between reporting years with documented support.
issue.why_triggered: Period normalization detected a bill crossing the reporting-year boundary and no allocation method in the workbook or evidence set.
issue.client_reported_basis_or_rationale: Full billed quantity entered in 2023 December workbook row.

source_locations.source_snippet: Service Period Dec 16, 2023 to Jan 15, 2024; Usage 32,800 MMBtu.

calculation_impact.impact_explanation: The 2023 portion cannot be re-performed until the allocation method is documented.

regulatory_citations.requirement_summary: Facility data should be reported for the appropriate annual reporting period.
regulatory_citations.applicability_explanation: The source service period crosses the 2023/2024 boundary.

regulatory_citations.requirement_summary: Annual reporting should align activity data to the relevant reporting year.
regulatory_citations.applicability_explanation: The workbook uses the bill as if it were a December-only record.

internal_basis: Formula library G-004 cutoff gap-ticket conversion

remediation.recommended_action: Apply a documented allocation method to split the Dec 16-Jan 15 bill between 2023 and 2024 and update the workbook period/quantity.
required_additional_evidence: Cross-year allocation worksheet or supplier daily usage detail
remediation.suggested_client_question: Please explain how the Dec 16, 2023–Jan 15, 2024 natural gas bill was allocated between reporting years.

title: GWP-100 applied where NY Part 253 GWP-20 basis is required

issue.observed_condition: The workbook labels CO2e columns as GWP-100 basis and uses CH4/N2O values consistent with a federal/100-year approach.
issue.expected_condition: For this NY Part 253 demo workflow, CO2e should use the configured NY GWP20 basis rather than federal/IPCC 100-year GWP values.
issue.why_triggered: The workbook basis conflicts with the engagement-required GWP basis; the formula library requires explicit NY-vs-federal GWP branch handling.
issue.client_reported_basis_or_rationale: Workbook flags GWP-100 basis in Natural Gas and Emissions Summary sheets.

upstream_rule_results.message: GWP basis conflicts with NY Part 253 demo configuration.

linked_workbook_locations.displayed_value: CO2e (metric tons) ⚠ GWP-100 APPLIED (NY req. GWP-20)
CO2e (metric tons) ⚠ GWP-100 BASIS (NY req. GWP-20)

reported_value.unit: metric tons CO2e on workbook GWP-100 basis

recalculated_value.unit: metric tons CO2e on NY GWP20 basis required

calculation_impact.impact_explanation: The final CO2e result cannot be considered NY-basis until CH4 and N2O are converted with the required GWP20 values.

regulatory_citations.requirement_summary: NY workflow requires the configured GWP20 basis for CO2e conversion.
regulatory_citations.applicability_explanation: The workbook uses a GWP-100 basis.

regulatory_citations.requirement_summary: CO2e conversion basis should follow NY-specific GWP treatment where applicable.
regulatory_citations.applicability_explanation: The demo is scoped to NY Part 253 rather than a generic federal Part 98 workbook.

internal_basis: Formula library Q-010 GWP basis consistency check
Formula library F-005 CO2e conversion branch

qualitative_factors: State-specific basis differs from federal default
materiality.rationale: GWP basis mismatch is a methodology-level error even before final numeric impact is recomputed.

remediation.recommended_action: Recalculate CO2e using the approved NY GWP20 set and disclose the GWP basis explicitly.
required_additional_evidence: Updated workbook formulas or calculation export
remediation.suggested_client_question: Please confirm the GWP basis used in the workbook and provide the NY GWP20 values/version applied for Part 253 reporting.
